title: Decouple Guardian transitions from Git state
goal: Allow hrn to advance Guardian workflows without user-created commits while preserving approval and verification gates and allowing optional autonomous Git control.

acceptanceCriteria.description: Guardian can select, approve, delegate, start, and complete eligible workflow transitions without a committed checkpoint, a Git repository, a commit, HEAD state, or a clean working tree.
Shared-understanding confirmation, manifest approval, dependency ordering, work-unit review evidence, and passing verification remain required.
Runtime policy and shipped guidance do not require user-created commits and do not prohibit the agent from using Git when appropriate.
Focused regressions for Git-independent Guardian behavior and the maintained npm test suite pass.

workUnits.title: Remove Git checkpoint gates from Guardian
workUnits.purpose: Eliminate Git commit and repository requirements from Guardian state transitions, workflow discovery, and shipped runtime guidance while retaining every non-Git evidence gate.
verification: Add and run focused regression coverage that exercises Guardian transitions without committed artifacts and outside a Git repository.
Run the maintained npm test suite after the focused checks.
stopConditions: Stop if a Git requirement is needed to preserve approval, verification, dependency, or review evidence.
Stop if the change would stage, commit, discard, or otherwise alter unrelated user changes.

workUnits.title: Independently verify Guardian evidence gates
workUnits.purpose: Adversarially review the integrated change and confirm that Git gates are removed without weakening non-Git workflow evidence requirements.
verification: Inspect the final diff and execute the focused Guardian regressions plus npm test.
Map every acceptance criterion to direct test or code-path evidence.
stopConditions: Stop and report any remaining Git-derived transition condition, any weakened non-Git evidence gate, or any failing maintained check.
